summary_of_threads_started_this_week:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin development community have focused on enhancing P2TR transactions by introducing data-carrying annexes to serve as authenticated payloads within accountable computing contracts, particularly for supervising AI agents. This innovation aims to ensure computational verifiability and accuracy in AI responses, addressing the principal-agent problem by potentially making AI errors economically penalizable using Bitcoin’s blockchain ([Source URL](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CALZpt+GGORG3bgM0C3sQYVNbc1W7aFyb0qP_c2xbZ8f64S_ksQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m49c2ecc7d787093a0fb97de67ccbedde0419eb51)).

In parallel, significant advancements have been made regarding Bitcoin's protocol, particularly in handling unstructured data within taproot annexes. A consensus on documenting a specific format `0x50 0x00` for such data aims to preserve the integrity of future transactions and prevent conflicts with future soft forks, thereby enhancing the reliability and future scalability of the Bitcoin network ([Delving Bitcoin on defining unstructured data](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/defining-0x50-0x00-as-unstructured-taproot-annex-data/2620)).

Additionally, the introduction of Fountain Codes represents a transformative approach to reducing blockchain storage costs by enabling nodes to reconstruct the entire blockchain from encoded segments. This method promises significant improvements in storage efficiency and network scalability, indicating a broader trend towards optimizing blockchain infrastructure for better performance and reduced resource demands ([Read more about Fountain Codes](https://lucasdbr05.com/posts/fountain-codes/)).

new_threads_this_week.title: Accountable Computing On-Chain Contracts for AI Agents Supervision
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin development focuses on data-carrying annexes for P2TR transactions.
- AI supervisory protocols could align incentives and conserve energy in critical applications.
- Bitcoin blockchain could transform global AI computation management and compensation.

new_threads_this_week.title: Defining 0x50 0x00 as unstructured taproot annex data
new_threads_this_week.summary: - The proposal seeks to standardize `0x50 0x00` as a marker for unstructured data in Bitcoin.  - This aims to preserve this format from being repurposed, avoiding future conflicts.  - Standardizing this data assists in mitigating potential adverse effects on future protocol interpretations.

new_threads_this_week.title: LND: Zero-Timestamp Gossip DoS disclosure
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- A significant LND vulnerability before v0.20.1 required network operators to update immediately.
- LND v0.20.1 now rejects zero-timestamp messages to prevent node crashes.
- Attackers could exploit this flaw by broadcasting specific zero-timestamp messages.

new_threads_this_week.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations, not Layer 1.
- Enhancements like CISA aim to optimize existing privacy measures economically.
- Regulatory compatibility influences Bitcoin's incremental privacy improvements.

new_threads_this_week.title: Privately running wallet privacy heuristics on your own UTXOs without needing to run your own node
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Recent tools highlight how blockchain transactions may compromise privacy.
- A new system allows private, node-independent analysis of transaction privacy.
- The system employs cryptographic techniques to ensure secure and private analyses.

new_threads_this_week.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Fountain Codes reduce Bitcoin blockchain storage needs by segmenting data into droplets.
- Research with Vinteum uses these codes to boost Bitcoin network efficiency and scalability.
- Detailed findings and methods are shared on the blog, including technical nuances.

new_threads_this_week.title: K-of-N threshhold key generation scheme by limited shared secrets
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Advanced cryptographic schemes improve multisignature arrangements and secure signing protocols.
- Novel methods enable creation of shared secrets enhancing the Lightning network's functionality.
- These cryptographic advancements strengthen security in decentralized digital transactions and blockchain applications.

new_threads_this_week.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Junhyuk Lee's paper discusses Bitcoin's security and mining profitability post-2140.
- Lee proposes mechanisms like base fees and adaptive block sizes to stabilize mining revenues.
- The research invites further scholarly discussion and can be accessed at the provided link.

active_posts_this_week.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- SPV protocol design concerns include vulnerability from fake connector outputs by miners.
- Proposals suggest invalidating 64-byte transactions to secure blockchain without altering SPV verifiers.
- Discussions emphasize trade-offs between security enhancements and increased system complexity.

active_posts_this_week.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts_this_week.summary: - The email explains disabling forks for Bitcoin and their timing related to quantum day.  - It discusses strategic implications of "Later" output types like P2TRv2 for Quantum Post-Quantum migration.  - It highlights the necessity for coordinated Bitcoin community actions against quantum threats.

active_posts_this_week.title: Proposal discussion: BIP39 native-language display wordlists
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Users often struggle with unlabelled seed phrases in cryptocurrency wallets.
- Proposed adjustments include encoding changes and increasing wordlist size for clearer differentiation.
- Strategies aim to improve security and aid users in correct seed phrase identification.

active_posts_this_week.title: [BIP Proposal] Removal of BIP 125 RBF signalling in wallet transactions
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The closure of a specific pull request was based on contributor feedback.
- Discussions occur on a platform for Bitcoin development, highlighting community involvement.
- Updates and discussions are disseminated through the "Bitcoin Development Mailing List."

active_posts_this_week.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The new cryptographic method modifies taproot, introducing "P2TRH" with a key recovery focus.
- It enhances efficiency by reducing space for public key verification without security risks.
- Notwithstanding, it poses security vulnerabilities especially if quantum decryption emerges.

active_posts_this_week.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Larger models over 30B parameters are preferred for Bitcoin's complex scripting needs.
- Effective datasets should index metadata like sources and dates for richer model insights.
- Benchmarking Bitcoin models involves specific questions on consensus rules and upgrades.

active_posts_this_week.title: Towards A K-of-N Lightning Network Node
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- A tweet sparked debate on using multiple shachains for blockchain security enhancement.
- Strategies include a formula for determining necessary shachains and signer knowledge configurations.
- Practical application involves integrating shachains with cryptographic frameworks like FROST.
